Live Pipeline in Action

Real terminal output, real logs, real decisions — every mechanism documented as it actually ran. Not mocked. Not simulated.

Persistent Memory Cache Hits Adaptive Fallback Structured Synthesis Fault Tolerance Cross-Paper Reasoning
01 / PLANNER AGENT
Query Decomposition

The Planner Agent receives a raw research topic and decomposes it into structured sub-questions and targeted search queries before any retrieval begins.

INPUT
Raw Topic
federated learning
→
01
Planner
decomposing
→
OUTPUT
5 Queries
structured plan
→
NEXT
Research
retrieval begins
RAW PLANNER OUTPUT — Structured Research Plan Generated Planner Agent
💡 The Planner generated 5 targeted search queries and 5 structured sub-questions from a single topic — "Federated Learning in Disease Diagnosis". Each query targets a distinct research angle: medical imaging, differential privacy, EHR analysis, rare disease, and comparative ML. This structured decomposition directly improves retrieval precision downstream.
02 / PERSISTENT MEMORY
Semantic Memory — Similarity Search & Regeneration Logic

ChromaDB + Sentence Transformers load on startup. The system searches memory for similar past work before generating anything. Low similarity triggers regeneration. High similarity triggers reuse.

0.138
Similarity Score
LOW
Threshold Result
↻
Action: Regenerate
103/103
Model Weights Loaded
VectorStore Init + Similarity Scores Memory Layer
🧠 ChromaDB initializes and sentence-transformers loads 103/103 weights. Similarity scores computed: 0.138 → below threshold → REGENERATE decision triggered.
LOW SIMILARITY → REGENERATE Adaptive Logic
⚙️ When similarity falls below the reuse threshold, the system automatically decides to regenerate rather than serve stale results. This is the core of the adaptive memory logic.
MEMORY RESULTS — Semantic Retrieval Returning Stored Summaries Memory Hit
💾 Memory returns previously stored paper summaries with full metadata — title, problem, method, key findings, limitations, confidence, quality score, source URL, and similarity score. This is persistent semantic retrieval working end-to-end — no API call needed for these papers.
03 / RELIABILITY ENGINEERING
Cache Hits · Groq Fallback · Rate Limit Handling

Two reliability mechanisms firing in real-time: in-session cache prevents redundant LLM calls, while Groq fallback automatically routes around Gemini failures and rate limits.

Cache Hit: Summary 2 & 3 — Then Groq Fallback on Rate Limit 429 Cache + Fallback
⚡ Cache hits on summaries 2 and 3 — no LLM call needed. On paper 4, Gemini returns 429 RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED. System immediately detects it, waits 60s, then auto-switches to Groq fallback — which succeeds. The pipeline never crashes.
Full Fallback Chain — Gemini Fails × 2 → Groq Fails → Critic Failed Gracefully Fault Tolerance
🛡️ Under a network failure scenario: Gemini retries twice → switches to Groq → Groq also fails → Critic retries 3 times → logs Critic failed gracefully. The pipeline does not crash — it degrades gracefully and continues. This is fault-tolerant orchestration, not just error handling.
04 / PIPELINE EXECUTION
End-to-End Pipeline Metrics

After retrieval and summarization complete, the pipeline emits structured quality metrics before advancing to synthesis. Every stage logs its output for observability.

Pipeline Metrics Logged → Synthesis Agent Starting Pipeline Orchestration
📊 Pipeline emits structured quality metrics after summarization: total papers, total summaries, avg relevance score (0.663), avg summary quality (8.4/10), and high confidence summary count (5/5). Then advances to Synthesis Agent.
Full Run Timeline — Plan Ready → Synthesis Metrics → Pipeline Complete Complete Run
05 / SYNTHESIS AGENT
Cross-Paper Reasoning Output

The Synthesis Agent produces structured JSON that goes beyond per-paper summarization — identifying common methods with source attribution, future directions with motivations, and a final synthesized insight.

Common Methods — Source-Attributed Synthesis
🔬 Federated Learning and Transfer Learning identified as common methods — each attributed to specific source papers. Not just labels — grounded in evidence.
Future Directions + Final Insight + Confidence Knowledge Synthesis
💡 Synthesis produces motivated future directions (not just labels), a final insight synthesizing the field, a confidence reasoning chain, and a confidence: high calibration. Synthesis metrics: 2 comparisons, 2 gaps, 2 trends.
